call for.example_sentence: This difficult task calls for a lot of patience.
call for.original_sentence: Big success calls for persons who continually set higher standards for themselves and others,

set higher standards.explanation_en: to demand better quality or performance
set higher standards.example_sentence: You should set higher standards for your school work.
set higher standards.original_sentence: Big success calls for persons who continually set higher standards for themselves and others,

efficiency.explanation_en: the ability to do things well without wasting time or energy
efficiency.example_sentence: Using a computer improves my work efficiency.
efficiency.original_sentence: persons who are searching for ways to increase efficiency, to get more output at lower cost,

output.explanation_en: the amount of something produced by a person, machine, or industry
output.example_sentence: The factory increased its daily output.
output.original_sentence: persons who are searching for ways to increase efficiency, to get more output at lower cost,

reserved for.explanation_en: kept or intended for a specific person or purpose
reserved for.example_sentence: This parking space is reserved for doctors.
reserved for.original_sentence: Top success is reserved for the I-can-do-it-better kind of person.

works magic.explanation_en: to have a very good or surprising effect
works magic.example_sentence: A little bit of kindness works magic in a relationship.

switched on.example_sentence: Once I start writing, my imagination is switched on.
switched on.original_sentence: Your creative power is switched on and ways for doing things better suggest themselves.

suggest themselves.explanation_en: to come into your mind automatically
suggest themselves.example_sentence: When you are relaxed, good ideas often suggest themselves.
suggest themselves.original_sentence: Your creative power is switched on and ways for doing things better suggest themselves.

devote.explanation_en: to give a lot of time or effort to something
devote.example_sentence: She devotes two hours a day to learning English.
devote.original_sentence: Each day, before you begin work, devote 10 minutes to thinking, how can I do a better job today?

get together.example_sentence: We usually get together with friends on weekends.
get together.original_sentence: Just about every time my wife and I would get together with a certain couple,

turn to.explanation_en: to change the subject of a conversation to something else
turn to.example_sentence: The conversation soon turned to politics.
turn to.original_sentence: the conversation would turn to working wives.

genuinely.example_sentence: I was genuinely surprised by the gift.
genuinely.original_sentence: Mrs. S had worked several years before her marriage, and she had genuinely liked it.

permanently.example_sentence: The accident left him permanently disabled.
permanently.original_sentence: Mr. S received a back injury that left him permanently disabled.

disabled.explanation_en: having an illness, injury, or condition that makes it difficult to do things
disabled.example_sentence: The building has facilities for disabled people.
disabled.original_sentence: Mr. S received a back injury that left him permanently disabled.

have no choice but to.explanation_en: to be forced to do something because there is no other option
have no choice but to.example_sentence: I had no choice but to walk home because the bus stopped running.
have no choice but to.original_sentence: Now Mrs. S had no choice but to go to work.

adjusted to.explanation_en: to become used to a new situation
adjusted to.example_sentence: It took me a while to adjust to the new time zone.
adjusted to.original_sentence: we were amazed to find how well she had adjusted to her new responsibilities.

make up my mind.example_sentence: I have made up my mind to study abroad next year.
make up my mind.original_sentence: But after the accident, I just made up my mind that I had to find the time.

conserve.explanation_en: to save or use something carefully so it is not wasted
conserve.example_sentence: We should try to conserve water during the summer.
conserve.original_sentence: I found dozens of ways to conserve time.

time killers.example_sentence: Social media can be one of the biggest time killers.
time killers.original_sentence: Fewer trips to the store, less TV, less telephoning, less of those time killers.

state of mind.explanation_en: a person's mood or mental attitude
state of mind.example_sentence: Your success depends on your positive state of mind.
state of mind.original_sentence: Capacity is a state of mind.
